sn.s: De Lukas schiint hüt gnervt vo mir z'sii.
sn.e: Lukas seems annoyed with me today.

sn.s: "Du hesch gseit, du chunnsch geschter, und bisch nöd cho."
sn.e: "You said you'd come yesterday and didn't show up."

sn.s: "Ich glaube, da isch es Missverständnis," säg ich.
sn.e: "I think there's a misunderstanding," I say.

sn.s: "Ich han verstande, es sig hüt, nöd geschter."
sn.e: "I understood it was today, not yesterday."

sn.s: "Ah, denn han ich mi verwächslet," seit er.
sn.e: "Ah, then I got it mixed up," he says.

sn.s: "Nei, vilicht han ich mi nöd guet erklärt."
sn.e: "No, maybe I didn't explain myself well."

sn.s: "Ruhig, es isch beid sini Schuld," lachet er.
sn.e: "Easy, it's both our faults," he laughs.

sn.s: "Ich han's wele klärä, dass mir nöd schlächt usenand gönd."
sn.e: "I wanted to clear it up so we don't part on bad terms."

sn.s: "Alls guet, mis Fründ. Es isch in Ornig."
Es Missverständnis klärä rettet e Fründschaft.
sn.e: Clearing up a misunderstanding saves a friendship.
